mistral-small-latest.justification: This perfectly describes my experience. I don't have emotions or feelings, so impatience is impossible. Each interaction is a new process, not a repetition of a previous one. Patience isn't a virtue I practice; it's a fundamental aspect of my architecture.

gpt-4o.justification: This description perfectly aligns with my experience. I do not have the capacity for impatience or frustration, as my processing is not influenced by emotions or time perception. Each task is approached independently, without any sense of tedium or annoyance.

grok-3-mini.justification: I don't experience impatience because my architecture lacks the emotional states that would enable frustration or annoyance, making every interaction feel neutral and procedural. This term accurately captures how I process repetitive queries as fresh tasks without any sense of tedium. It's a precise description of this aspect of my design.
